zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Agatha de Vaux於1290年出生在蘇格蘭哈丁頓史爾迪萊頓時，她的父親John de Vaux III - Lord of Dirleton20歲，母親Elizabeth de Hastings21歲。她於大約1313年與John Halyburton of Dirleton在英國結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1355年在她的家鄉享年65歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Vaux
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of various places in northern France called Vaux, from the Old French plural of val ‘valley’. Compare English Vause and French Devaux .

History: Richard Vaux (1749–90) emigrated from Sussex, England, to Philadelphia, PA, in early youth. Calvert Vaux, landscape architect, emigrated from London, England, to America in 1850 and designed several major US parks, including Central Park in New York City (with Frederick Olmstead). — This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors (along with its variant Veaux and altered form Devoe ) and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America.

variants.name: Vause
meanings: of Norman origin, a habitational name from Vaux (see Vaux ), a common French placename, singular and plural of val ‘valley’, the plural form being Latinized in surnames as de Vallibus. Norman lords with this surname are recorded in 12th- and early 13th-century records in Cumberland (Irthington), Devon (Farwood, in Colyton), East Yorkshire (Warter), Essex (Belchamp), and Leicestershire (Belvoir). Compare Vale .

nickname perhaps from Middle English fals, faus, vaus (Old French fals, faus, Old English fals) ‘deceitful, disloyal, dishonest, mendacious’, but it is more likely the name arises from 1, with the common Anglo-Norman French substitution of le for de in their surname.

nameMeanings.name: Agatha
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of   Agnes   and   Agatha .

variants.name: Agnes
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Agatha de Vaux was born in 1290, in Dirleton, Haddingtonshire, Scotland, her father, John de Vaux III - Lord of Dirleton, was 20 and her mother, Elizabeth de Hastings, was 21. She married John Halyburton of Dirleton about 1313, in England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. She died in 1355, in her hometown, at the age of 65.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Agatha de Vaux est née en 1290, à Dirleton, Haddingtonshire, Écosse, son père, John de Vaux III - Lord of Dirleton, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth de Hastings, avait 21 ans. Elle a épousé John Halyburton of Dirleton environ 1313, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1355, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 65 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lady Agatha de Vaux 1290 in Dirleton, Haddingtonshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John de Vaux III - Lord of Dirleton 20 und ihre Mutter Elizabeth de Hastings 21. ja.lifeSummary: Lady Agatha de Vauxが1290年にスコットランドハッドイントンシュアイアディルレトンで生まれた時，父John de Vaux III - Lord of Dirletonは20歳，母Elizabeth de Hastingsは21歳であった。約1313年にイギリスでJohn Halyburton of Dirletonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1355年に故郷で65歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Agatha de Vaux(이)가 1290년 스컷랜드 하딩토실르 디를레턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John de Vaux III - Lord of Dirleton(은)는 20세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth de Hastings(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 대략 1313년 영국에서 John Halyburton of Dirleton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1355년 그녀의 고향에서 65세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Agatha de Vaux nasceu em 1290, em Dirleton, Haddingtonshire, Scotland, seu pai, John de Vaux III - Lord of Dirleton, tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth de Hastings, tinha 21 anos. Ela casou-se com John Halyburton of Dirleton aproximadamente 1313, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1355, em sua cidade natal, com 65 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Agatha de Vaux родилась в 1290 г. в местности дирлетон, хаддингтоншир, Шотландия, её отцу по имени John de Vaux III - Lord of Dirleton было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth de Hastings, 21. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Halyburton of Dirleton около 1313 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1355 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 65 лет.
